title: Write AGENTS.md for agent-session grounding
description: One-page AGENTS.md at repo root: what volley is (adversarial plan/critique loop, file-state, verdict protocol), the load-bearing invariants an agent must not break (machine-readable VERDICT last line; files are the only state; critic read-only; billing guard; role pinning; no stall detection BY OWNER DECISION — do not re-add), pointers to prompts/ and the mock-testing pattern (role-aware mocks + matrix under both VOLLEY_PLANNER values), and the bead workflow (br/bv) for planned work. Rationale: every future agent session re-derives this context today; several beads in this set assume a shared grounding doc exists.

title: Volley archive and index
description: volley-archive <workspace>: tarball the workspace into archives/YYYY-MM-DD-<brief-slug>.tgz and append a row (date, brief title, planner, rounds, outcome, path) to archives/index.md. Rationale: finished volleys are decision records; today they rot in scratch dirs — the e2e2/e2e3 runs from this project's own development are already only in /tmp.

title: Orchestrator stage support (--stage build, volley-build wrapper)
description: volley.sh: VOLLEY_STAGE=plan|build (default plan), pinned in state like roles. Build stage requires an approved plan (state contains ratified/approved marker or SPEC.md + explicit --force-build). Stage selects prompt set, rounds dir (build-rounds/), log names, round cap (BUILD_MAX_ROUNDS), and planner invocation variant: claude planner gains Bash in allowedTools for build only; codex planner unchanged (workspace-write already permits execution). SAFETY NOTE: claude Bash is NOT OS-sandboxed the way codex workspace-write is — README must state that build stage grants the claude planner shell access, and recommend planner=codex for stricter isolation. volley-build wrapper = exec volley.sh with VOLLEY_STAGE=build. Convergence and impasse semantics identical to plan stage. ACCEPTANCE: plan-stage behavior is byte-identical when VOLLEY_STAGE unset (regression guard via existing mock matrix).

title: Build-stage prompts: implementer-init/revise, code-critic
description: Write prompts/implementer-init.md (implement SPEC.md exactly; run the spec's Testing Strategy; write state/test-results.txt with each command, exit code, and trimmed output; record deviations in BUILD-NOTES.md; never edit SPEC.md), prompts/implementer-revise.md (address each objection by ID; fix or rebut into BUILD-NOTES.md Decision Log; rerun tests; refresh test-results.txt), prompts/code-critic.md (read spec, code, test-results, BUILD-NOTES, and — when git trail exists — the diff since the previous critique; severity-tagged objections; missing/failing test-results.txt is an automatic BLOCKER; style is explicitly out of scope; same VERDICT final line). Keep role voice consistent with existing prompts.

title: Build stage: volley implements the approved spec
description:
BACKGROUND: volley currently ends at an approved SPEC.md. The same adversarial mechanism — planner acts, critic gates with machine-readable verdicts, files carry all state — applies unchanged to producing working code. This is the leap from planning tool to autonomous dev pipeline.

GOAL: 'volley-build' wrapper (and --stage build in volley.sh): the planner implements SPEC.md inside the workspace (src/ plus whatever the spec dictates), RUNS the spec's own Testing Strategy, and writes results (commands, exit codes, trimmed output) to state/test-results.txt plus BUILD-NOTES.md for any deviation from spec. The critic reviews the code and results READ-ONLY — it never executes anything — with a code rubric: defect = code contradicts the spec, fails its own recorded tests, or misses a required edge case; style is out of scope. Same VERDICT protocol, separate BUILD_MAX_ROUNDS (default 8), rounds in build-rounds/ so plan history is preserved.

KEY DESIGN DECISIONS (settled here to avoid relitigating): critic stays read-only and trusts the recorded test artifact BUT must treat a missing or failing test-results.txt as a BLOCKER, which keeps the planner honest without giving the critic execution rights. Planner permissions: claude gets Bash added to allowed tools ONLY in build stage (it must run tests); codex planner already executes under workspace-write. Stage is pinned in state alongside roles.

DEPENDENCIES (hard, reflected in bead deps): severity-tiers — code review without severity discipline will not converge, style nits are endless; git layer — per-round commits are the diff medium the code critic reviews ('review the diff since your last critique' keeps later rounds cheap and focused).

WHY CONFIDENT: every mechanism transfers; both agents demonstrably write and revise real artifacts in this harness (four live runs). Risk is convergence discipline on code, which is exactly what the two dependency epics supply.

title: Test-results contract enforcement
description: Orchestrator-side guard rails for the artifact the critic trusts: after each implementer call in build stage, verify state/test-results.txt exists and was modified during that call (mtime vs a marker); if not, inject a one-shot reminder invocation (mirrors the missing-verdict retry pattern) before handing to the critic; log the anomaly. Never parse test outcomes in the orchestrator — judging results is the critic's job (per epic design decision); the orchestrator only guarantees the artifact's existence/freshness. ACCEPTANCE: mock implementer that 'forgets' the artifact triggers exactly one reminder then proceeds.

title: Verify build stage: mock plumbing + live datestamp pipeline
description: Mocks: stage pinning, prompt-set selection, build-rounds/ separation, artifact-freshness reminder, regression: full existing plan-stage matrix must still pass untouched. LIVE e2e: datestamp brief through plan stage (should approve in ~1 round) then volley-build; assert the produced script exists, is executable, running ./datestamp emits ISO-8601 UTC and exits 0 (verify by executing it ourselves in the test harness — do not trust state/test-results.txt for this assertion), and that state/test-results.txt matches observed reality. Then one live build on a meatier brief if budget allows. PASS/FAIL logging throughout.

title: CI mode (GitHub Action)
description: Action wrapper: on PRs touching briefs/, run volley in the runner, upload workspace as artifact, post REPORT.md summary as a PR comment. Requires deliberate billing decision (runners have no consumer subscriptions => VOLLEY_ALLOW_API_KEY=1 with metered keys, documented loudly, or self-hosted runner with logins). Rationale: makes volley a team artifact rather than a laptop tool; the billing guard's override exists precisely for this consciously-chosen case.

title: Closing pass: planner addresses non-blocking remarks after APPROVE
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner. This bead replaces the deleted severity-tiers epic after owner review found it heavier than the observed problem.

OBSERVED BEHAVIOR IT AUTOMATES (owner ground truth from manual volleying): frontier critics already self-tag "non-blocking minor" remarks in their approving round, unprompted; the owner routinely asks the planner to respond to those non-blockers anyway before closing, siding sometimes with planner and sometimes with critic. No imposed taxonomy needed — the critic natural labeling is the input.

MECHANISM: when the critic verdict is APPROVE, do not exit immediately. Run ONE final planner invocation with prompts/closing-pass.md: "the critic approved but noted non-blocking remarks in rounds/rNN.critique.md; address each one in SPEC.md or consciously decline it with a Decision Log entry; change nothing else." Then exit 0. If the approving critique contains no non-blocking remarks, skip the pass (cheap grep for the critic remark labeling; if ambiguous, run the pass anyway — the planner declining vacuously is harmless). Config: VOLLEY_CLOSING_PASS=1 default, 0 disables.

ALSO: one sentence added to prompts/critic.md blessing the labeling convention the critic already uses ("you may note non-blocking remarks alongside an APPROVE; label them clearly") so the closing-pass input stays reliable.

SCOPE GUARDS: no severity definitions, no objection IDs, no orchestrator parsing of tiers, no Accepted Risks appendix — all explicitly rejected as heavier than the problem. The closing pass planner call does NOT trigger a re-review; critic approval stands.

TESTS: mocks — approve-with-remarks triggers exactly one extra planner call then exit 0; approve-clean skips it; VOLLEY_CLOSING_PASS=0 skips it; REVISE rounds unaffected; full existing matrix regression. Live: one run on a brief known to produce closing remarks; verify Decision Log entries for each remark. PASS/FAIL logging.
notes: Implemented (commit pending push): closing_pass() in volley.sh, prompts/closing-pass.md, critic.md labeling sentence, README knob row. Mock matrix 59/59 incl. approve-with-remarks (one extra planner call, critic not re-run), approve-clean skip, VOLLEY_CLOSING_PASS=0 skip, REVISE-round regression. REMAINING: live run on a remark-producing brief; will share the live run with volley-second-opinion-rab (second opinion feeds the same closing pass).

title: Dual-run comparison harness (local-model experiment bench)
description:
STATUS: APPROVED-DEFERRED by owner; implement alongside/after pluggable agents (volley-pluggable-agents-5dp). NOT default behavior — a separate opt-in wrapper, never wired into plain volley runs. This is Rung 2 of the tournament-mode review, RESTRUCTURED per owner acceptance: its purpose is an EXPERIMENT HARNESS for comparing agent pairings (primarily local models), not a spec-quality feature. Synthesis (auto-merging the two specs) was REJECTED permanently: the owner prefers to exercise taste at merge points, and per-topic model merging risks incoherent hybrids; do not re-propose.

MECHANISM: volley-compare <workspace>: seeds N sub-workspaces (v1: two, A/ and B/) from the same BRIEF.md, each with a configured planner/critic pairing (env or simple config: A=claude/codex, B=ollama:qwen/claude, etc.); runs them concurrently; waits; emits COMPARISON.md — outcome per branch (converged/impasse, rounds, wall time; telemetry columns when VOLLEY_TELEMETRY=1), plus a machine-written divergence summary of where the approved specs differ (one agent invocation reading both specs; descriptive only, no winner-picking). The human picks or merges — deliberately.

IMPASSE: branches are independent; report each branch outcome as-is. Brief-hash guard so a workspace cannot mix runs of different briefs.

TESTS: mocks — both-converge, one-impasse, both-impasse; divergence summary invocation receives both specs; guard trips on brief change. Live: one comparison run (cloud pairings suffice until pluggable agents lands). PASS/FAIL logging.

title: Fresh-eyes ratification pass after APPROVE
description:
BACKGROUND: each critic call is context-fresh (stateless design) but always reads the Decision Log, which anchors it to the debate's framing. Approval after N rounds therefore risks convergence-by-attrition, and a spec can pass while only making sense to someone who watched the argument.

GOAL: after the incumbent critic's APPROVE, run exactly one ratification review: same critic agent, but its input is SPEC.md with the Decision Log and Accepted Risks sections STRIPPED, and a dedicated prompt (prompts/ratify.md): 'you have never seen this project; is this spec complete, self-contained, and buildable exactly as written?' Same VERDICT protocol. APPROVE => log 'ratified' and exit 0. REVISE => its critique becomes the next rounds/rNN.critique.md and the loop continues normally (counts against MAX_ROUNDS). Config: VOLLEY_RATIFY=1 default, 0 disables. Livelock guard: at most one ratification cycle per run — a second incumbent APPROVE exits without re-ratifying (log states this).

WHY CONFIDENT: costs one extra agent call; directly tests the property users care about (spec stands alone); the stripped-log trick reuses every existing mechanism. This is the cheapest large trust win available.

title: Git-native workspace audit trail + REPORT.md generator
description:
BACKGROUND: workspace history is cp snapshots (rounds/rNN.spec.md) plus logs. No diffs between rounds, no provenance, no single artifact a human can read end-to-end. The debate IS the product for a human reviewer, and today it is scattered across five files.

GOAL: (a) auto 'git init' inside the workspace (guard: skip if workspace is already inside a git repo, to avoid nested-repo surprises; then fall back to cp snapshots); one commit per event — initial draft, each critique, each revision — with structured messages like 'r02 revise: fixed r02-1, rebutted r02-2', author identity 'volley planner/critic <volley@local>'; tag final commit 'approved' or 'impasse'. (b) generate REPORT.md at exit: brief, outcome, per-round sections (critique text, spec diffstat, key Decision Log entries), Accepted Risks, timings from volley.log. Pure shell + git log/diff; zero new dependencies.

WHY CONFIDENT: git gives diffing/bisect/provenance for free and replaces hand-rolled snapshotting; every consumer of volley output so far (this project's own sessions) had to reconstruct the debate manually from rounds/*.

DESIGN NOTES: keep rounds/*.critique.md files (they are the critic-output capture medium); rounds/*.spec.md cp snapshots retained in v1 for grep-ability, marked deprecated in README once git trail is proven. REPORT.md is regenerable at any time from workspace state (idempotent script, callable standalone as volley-report).

title: Workspace git layer: init guard, per-event commits, tags
description: Add to volley.sh: ensure_git() — if git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree fails for the workspace, git init -b volley + identity config local to repo (user.name 'volley', user.email 'volley@local'); if workspace IS already in a repo, set GIT_TRAIL=off and log it (never commit into a user's repo uninvited). commit_event(role, msg): git add -A + commit with '[rNN] role: msg'. Call after initial draft, after each critique capture, after each revision. Tag 'volley-approved' or 'volley-impasse' at exit. ACCEPTANCE: mock run yields expected commit sequence and tag; pre-existing-repo workspace yields zero commits and a logged notice.

title: Verify git trail + report: mock assertions and live regeneration
description: Mock matrix additions: assert commit count = 1 (draft) + 2 per round (critique, revision) minus final round's revision on approve; assert tag exists and matches exit code; assert nested-repo guard (run workspace inside a temp git repo, expect no commits + notice). Live: regenerate REPORT.md for a fresh live run and for the historical e2e3 workspace (no git trail — exercises degraded mode). Test scripts log every assertion PASS/FAIL.

title: Write HACKING.md: maintainer briefing, explicitly pointed-to only
description:
STATUS: redesigned per owner ruling 2026-07-09. The file must NOT be named AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md): magic filenames are auto-ingested by agent runtimes with differing agendas — concretely, volley own codex critic runs with cwd=workspace, so a volley run in the repo directory would silently inject maintainer policy into an unrelated critique. The name must be one no runtime auto-reads: HACKING.md (venerable convention for "notes for those modifying this software"; owner may prefer another name — confirm before creating).

DISCOVERY IS EXPLICIT, NEVER AMBIENT: referenced from README design-notes section ("modifying volley? read HACKING.md first") and pasted/pointed-to deliberately when a dev session starts. No hook, no auto-load.

CONTENT (one page): (1) what volley is, three sentences; (2) load-bearing invariants a change must not break, each with its reason: machine-readable VERDICT last line (sole termination signal), files as only state (crash-resume depends on it), critic read-only under both role assignments, role pinning, stdin </dev/null and nested-env guards (each earned by a real bug), billing guard as product property; (3) OWNER RULINGS — rejected ideas, do not re-propose: build/implementation stage (volley plans, never builds), stall detection, severity taxonomy/objection IDs/Accepted Risks appendix, spec synthesis/auto-merge (owner keeps merge taste), git-audit trail + report generator, fresh-eyes ratification, packaging, CI mode, notifications, archive; optional features default OFF; subscriptions-only billing. (4) verification doctrine: role-aware mocks, matrix under both VOLLEY_PLANNER values, live half-test before full e2e; (5) work tracking: beads via br/bv, owner approval precedes implementation. MAINTENANCE RULE: a ruling not added when made is lost — updating this file is part of recording any future owner decision.

title: HUMAN.md steering channel
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner for implementation.

If workspace/HUMAN.md exists when a round starts, both role prompts include it as a directive block ("the human left instructions — they outrank the critic"), then it is archived to rounds/rNN.human.md and removed so it applies exactly once. Design question settled during review: the directive should also silence re-litigation of any point it settles (one line in the critic prompt). Preserves the no-input-required contract while allowing a human to steer a long run without killing it; the only current steering option is ^C, which discards an in-flight round.

title: Completion notifications (macOS + webhook)
description: On converge/impasse: osascript display notification with outcome and round count (darwin only, guarded), plus optional POST to VOLLEY_WEBHOOK_URL with {outcome, rounds, workspace}. Rationale: volleys run 5-25 minutes unattended; the human context-switches away and currently has to poll.

title: Packaging: installer, volley new scaffold, man page
description: install.sh (symlink volley.sh + wrappers into ~/.local/bin), Homebrew tap formula, 'volley new <dir>' scaffolding (BRIEF.md template with prompts for goals/constraints/non-goals), man page generated from README. Rationale: current install is 'clone and cd'; distribution polish is what turns a personal script into a tool others adopt.

title: Pluggable agent adapters (config-driven registry)
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner; deferred for now BUT strategically important — owner is increasingly involved in LOCAL MODELS and anticipates running volley experiments against combinations of them (e.g. planner=local-model, critic=claude, and local-vs-local). Design with that as the primary target, not hypothetical third-party cloud CLIs.

Generalize the four per-agent functions into an adapter contract: an agent = {name, plan_fn, critique_fn} defined in sourced config (agents.d/*.sh), each fn honoring the (prompt[, capture-file]) signature and the workspace-only-writes rule. VOLLEY_PLANNER/VOLLEY_CRITIC then name any registered agent. Local-model paths to evaluate when implementing: codex exec --oss --local-provider lmstudio|ollama (already installed, zero new deps), plus a direct ollama/lmstudio adapter for models codex cannot front. Billing guard: local adapters trivially satisfy it (no billable credential at all) — guard becomes per-adapter hook. Keep claude+codex as built-ins/defaults; behavior without config must remain byte-identical.

CAVEAT RECORDED FROM SEVERITY-TIERS REVIEW (owner decision 2026-07-09): the full severity-tier design (BLOCKER/MAJOR/MINOR tags with definitions, stable objection IDs, evidence requirements per tier, orchestrator tier parsing) was rejected as heavier than the problem FOR FRONTIER MODELS, which self-tag non-blocking remarks naturally. If local-model critics prove noisy or unlabeled in these experiments, that design is the known remedy — the design discussion lives in the volley project session history and can be re-derived cheaply. Watch for: unlabeled objection spray, no self-triage in approving rounds, relitigation of settled points.

title: Ratify hook: stripped-spec input, prompt, one-cycle guard
description: Implement in volley.sh: on APPROVE, if VOLLEY_RATIFY=1 and not already ratified this run (state/ratified marker), write state/spec-for-ratify.md = SPEC.md minus '## Decision Log' and '## Accepted Risks' sections (awk section stripper), invoke critic with prompts/ratify.md pointing it at that file explicitly (critic is read-only; the file lives in the workspace so both agents can read it). Log 'ratifying with fresh eyes' / 'ratified' / 'ratification raised objections — continuing'. Write prompts/ratify.md per epic wording, same VERDICT contract, severity tags per severity-tiers epic if landed (soft coupling: works with or without tiers). ACCEPTANCE: both verdict outcomes drive the documented control flow.

title: Verify ratification: mock outcomes + one live ratified run
description: Mock scenarios: (a) ratify-approve => exit 0, log shows ratified, exactly 1 extra critic call; (b) ratify-revise-then-approve => loop continued, second APPROVE exits WITHOUT second ratification (livelock guard), marker file present; (c) VOLLEY_RATIFY=0 => zero extra calls. Assert section-stripper correctness on a spec containing decoy headings (e.g. '### Decision Log rationale' inside another section must survive). Live: rerun datestamp brief end-to-end with ratification on; confirm ratified in log. PASS/FAIL logging per assertion.

title: Volley against an existing repo (auto-brief context)
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner for implementation.

Allow the workspace to reference an existing codebase read-only: VOLLEY_CONTEXT_DIR=/abs/path given to both agents (claude: --add-dir; codex: verify read scope of the read-only sandbox outside the workspace empirically before relying on it). BRIEF.md can then request plans about real systems ("plan the migration of X in this repo") and both planner and critic ground in actual code. Writes remain confined to the workspace — volley stays strictly on the planning side. MITIGATION for context dilution: require BRIEF.md to name entry points/paths of interest; prompt line tells agents the context dir exists and is read-only. Guard: path must be absolute, readable, and outside the workspace.

title: REPORT.md generator (standalone, idempotent)
description: New script volley-report (also invoked automatically at loop exit): reads BRIEF.md, state/volley.log, rounds/*, git log/diff when trail exists. Structure: title/outcome/roles/timings table; per round: verdict + tier counts, critique inlined, 'spec changes' as git diffstat (full diff in a details block); Decision Log and Accepted Risks copied verbatim; footer with round count and wall time. Must degrade gracefully without git trail (omit diffs, note why). ACCEPTANCE: report generated for an archived scratch workspace (e2e2) reads coherently top-to-bottom with zero manual edits.

title: Critic rubric profiles (--profile security|ux|data)
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner for implementation.

Optional prompt fragments prompts/profiles/<name>.md concatenated into the rendered critic prompt when VOLLEY_PROFILE=<name> is set; no profile => byte-identical current behavior. A fragment does three things: (1) extends the material-defect list for the domain, (2) gives the critic an active probe list (check even if the brief is silent — the silence itself can be the objection), (3) calibrates severity for the domain (auto-MAJOR items when severity tiers land; composes with tiers but requires neither). Ship exactly three curated fragments and stop until real use demands more: security (trust boundaries, unstated validation, secrets lifetime, injection surfaces — motivating example: five critique rounds on the queuectl brief and no critic ever flagged that the tool executes attacker-supplied strings with no stated trust model), data (consistency, migration, loss), decision-memo (unstated assumptions as facts, options compared on inconsistent criteria, unflagged irreversibility, costless assertions, unconsidered stakeholders — this is the fragment that makes volley competent on NON-SOFTWARE briefs, an owner priority). GUARD against checklist zealotry: probes remain subject to the existing materiality bar, stated in each fragment. Implementation: ~10 lines in volley.sh (validate fragment exists, append at render), the fragments, mock-matrix coverage.

title: Schema-enforced verdicts (retire grep + retry path)
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner, explicitly deprioritized to END of queue (owner decision, contingent on the effort estimate ~1 day being accurate).

Use codex exec --output-schema to make {verdict, objections[]} structurally guaranteed on the codex side, eliminating the missing-verdict re-ask for it; claude side stays convention-plus-parsing (extract from JSON output field), so the grep fallback remains. Net effect: removes machinery (the retry path) rather than adding it, but the win is asymmetric — half the benefit. Includes reworking mocks to emit schema output. Do LAST among approved work.

title: Second-opinion pass: swapped critic reviews the final spec
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner. NOT default behavior — VOLLEY_SECOND_OPINION=1 enables, unset/0 = no change to current flow. This bead is Rung 1 of the tournament-mode review; the full tournament (parallel branches + synthesis) was rejected, synthesis permanently (owner accepted recommendation 2026-07-09).

MOTIVATION (measured, not hypothetical): critic identity has demonstrated asymmetry — in live runs on the same brief, claude-as-critic produced 3 deep objections with failure scenarios per round vs codex-as-critic 1/round — so a single volley inherits one critic blind spots. This pass gets the other model eyes on the final artifact for one extra call.

MECHANISM: after the incumbent critic APPROVEs, invoke the OTHER agent critique function once against SPEC.md (same critic prompt, read-only, same capture path) writing rounds/second-opinion.md. Its remarks feed the closing pass (volley-closing-pass-68o): planner addresses or consciously declines each into the Decision Log. No verdict authority — the second opinion cannot flip APPROVE to REVISE (it is input to the closing pass, not a gate); this keeps it lightweight and non-recursive. Log clearly: "second opinion from <agent>: N remarks".

ORDERING: runs BEFORE the closing pass so one closing pass disposes of both critics leftovers in a single call. Depends on closing-pass bead landing first.

TESTS: mocks — enabled: exactly one extra critique call by the non-incumbent agent + closing pass covers its remarks; disabled: byte-identical current flow; impasse path unaffected (no second opinion on impasse). Live: one run with it enabled; verify Decision Log entries reference second-opinion remarks. PASS/FAIL logging.
notes: Implemented in volley.sh (second_opinion() + SECOND_FN/SECOND_AGENT role wiring); mocks green 83/83 (enabled path, non-incumbent reviewer, one closing pass covers both critics, clean-clean skip, impasse unaffected, default-off byte-identical). Commit 3e8f993. REMAINING: live run in flight (background task, snapshot of 3e8f993, VOLLEY_SECOND_OPINION=1, brief=linecount CLI); on completion verify rounds/second-opinion.md + Decision Log dispositions, then close along with volley-closing-pass-68o.

title: Orchestrator: parse tier counts, log them per round
description: In volley.sh after verdict parse, count objections per tier in the critique file (grep -c '\[BLOCKER\]' etc.) and log e.g. 'r03: verdict is REVISE (1 blocker, 2 major, 3 minor)'. If verdict is REVISE with zero blockers/majors, log a rubric-violation warning but do NOT overrule the critic in v1 (observability first, enforcement later, so we can see how often agents violate the rule before automating trust). ACCEPTANCE: counts appear in state/volley.log for every round; anomaly warning fires on a synthetic rubric-violating critique.

title: Prompts: severity format, verdict rule, Accepted Risks flow
description: Update prompts/critic.md: objection format 'N. [SEVERITY] (rNN-K) title', severity definitions (see epic), and the structural verdict rule (REVISE iff any BLOCKER/MAJOR; MINORs alone => APPROVE with MINORs listed for the record). Update prompts/planner-revise.md: fix or rebut every BLOCKER/MAJOR into the Decision Log referencing objection IDs; move MINORs to '## Accepted Risks' appendix (objection ID + one-line risk statement) unless trivially fixable in-place. planner-init.md: create empty Accepted Risks section alongside Decision Log. ACCEPTANCE: a live critic call emits tagged, ID'd objections; a live revise call produces Decision Log entries citing IDs and an Accepted Risks appendix.

title: Verify severity tiers: mock matrix + live comparison run
description: Extend scratch mocks to emit tagged critiques (converge2 mode: r1 = 1 MAJOR, r2 = 2 MINOR + APPROVE). Assert: tier counts in volley.log, Accepted Risks appendix created by mock-planner path stays coherent, verdict paths unchanged (converge/noverdict/impasse/resume under both VOLLEY_PLANNER values). Then one LIVE run on the job-queue brief; record rounds-to-converge vs the historical baseline (3 rounds planner=claude, 2 rounds planner=codex, from state logs of e2e2/e2e3 scratch runs) and confirm the appendix contains real MINORs. All test scripts must log each assertion with PASS/FAIL lines.

title: Severity-tiered structured critiques (BLOCKER/MAJOR/MINOR)
description:
BACKGROUND: volley converges when the critic emits VERDICT: APPROVE. Today an objection is an unstructured numbered list item, and only rubric prose restrains nitpicking. The core failure mode of LLM critics is inflating minor preferences into round-burning objections; we currently fight it rhetorically, not structurally.

GOAL: every objection carries a severity tag and a stable ID: 'N. [BLOCKER|MAJOR|MINOR] (rNN-K) title' + body. Verdict rule becomes structural: REVISE if and only if at least one BLOCKER or MAJOR remains. MINORs are recorded into a '## Accepted Risks' appendix of SPEC.md instead of forcing revision rounds. Decision Log entries reference objection IDs, enabling later tooling (re-litigation detection, cross-round diffing) without committing to it now.

SEVERITY DEFINITIONS (to be embedded in prompts): BLOCKER = spec is wrong or unbuildable as written; MAJOR = a BRIEF.md requirement unmet or an internal contradiction; MINOR = an improvement that does not threaten correctness or feasibility.

WHY CONFIDENT: observed in live runs (job-queue brief, 3 rounds; swapped run, 2 rounds) that objection quality varies and rubric prose is the only brake. Tiering shortens volleys, makes every remaining round substantive, and produces an Accepted Risks record humans actually want.

SCOPE: prompts + orchestrator logging only; verdict grep protocol unchanged. No stall detection (explicitly excluded by project owner).

title: Per-run telemetry and cost summary
description:
STATUS: APPROVED by owner with a binding constraint: STRICTLY OPT-IN, default OFF (VOLLEY_TELEMETRY=1 enables; unset/0 = zero telemetry code paths run, no metrics files created).

REFRAMED VALUE (owner input): not cost anxiety — subscriptions pick up the tab and owner is explicitly unworried. The real use is as a measurement instrument for LOCAL-MODEL experiments (see volley-pluggable-agents-5dp): comparing model combinations on wall time per call, tokens per round, and rounds-to-converge per planner/critic pairing. Design the metrics schema with cross-run comparison in mind (state/metrics.tsv columns: round, role, agent/adapter name, model, wall seconds, tokens in/out where the CLI reports them), since the point is lining up runs side by side, not auditing one run. claude -p --output-format json and codex --json provide token counts for the cloud agents; local adapters report what their runtime exposes (ollama exposes eval counts). End-of-run one-line summary printed only when enabled. SEQUENCING: most of its value unlocks once pluggable agents lands; fine to implement earlier since it measures cloud agents identically.

title: Tournament mode: both directions in parallel + synthesis
description:
BACKGROUND: planner direction is configurable and live runs exposed asymmetry — Claude-as-critic produced materially deeper critiques (3 detailed objections w/ failure scenarios) than Codex-as-critic (1/round); each planner also drafts differently. For high-stakes briefs, one direction leaves value on the table.

GOAL: volley-tournament <workspace>: run cc-volley and codex-volley concurrently in sub-workspaces A/ (claude plans) and B/ (codex plans) from the same BRIEF.md. When both converge: cross-review — each agent critiques the OTHER side approved spec once (severity-tagged); then a synthesis invocation (default planner: claude, configurable) merges both specs + both cross-reviews into SPEC-FINAL.md, choosing the stronger treatment per topic and recording every choice in a "## Synthesis Log".

FAILURE HANDLING: one side impasses => proceed with the survivor (skip cross-review of the dead branch, note it in Synthesis Log); both impasse => tournament impasse, both IMPASSE.md files surfaced.

COST: ~2x a single volley plus 3 calls — acceptable by owner stated indifference to plan-quota usage; document in README regardless.

STATUS: idea approved for further consideration by owner (not yet greenlit for implementation).

title: Parallel runner: sub-workspaces, concurrency, impasse handling
description: volley-tournament script: seed A/ and B/ each with BRIEF.md copy; launch both volley.sh runs backgrounded with distinct logs; wait on both PIDs; collect exit codes. Exit-code matrix per epic (0/0 => synthesize; 0/2 => survivor path; 2/2 => tournament impasse exit 2). Combined progress log state/tournament.log tails both children's volley.log with A:/B: prefixes. Guard: workspace must not already contain A/ or B/ from a different brief (hash BRIEF.md into state).

title: Cross-review + synthesis prompts and orchestration
description: prompts/cross-review.md (critique the other side approved spec; you have not seen its debate; severity tags; no verdict line needed — this is input, not a gate) and prompts/synthesize.md (merge two approved specs + two cross-reviews into SPEC-FINAL.md; per-topic choice with Synthesis Log entries naming which side won and why; do not average — pick and justify). Orchestrate: two cross-review calls (each agent reviews opposite spec), then one synthesis call producing SPEC-FINAL.md. ACCEPTANCE: SPEC-FINAL.md exists with populated Synthesis Log.

title: Verify tournament: mock matrix + one live tournament
description: Mocks: all three exit-code combinations; brief-hash guard; prefixed combined log. Assert survivor path skips dead-branch cross-review. LIVE: one full tournament on the job-queue brief; manually spot-check that the Synthesis Log's choices reference real differences between A and B specs (not confabulated). Compare SPEC-FINAL.md quality against the historical single-direction specs (e2e2, e2e3) as a qualitative note in the test log. PASS/FAIL logging.

title: Recreate mock test harness and baseline matrix
description: The mock claude/codex binaries and the volley.sh test matrix from the initial build session were never committed; every approved p1 bead test plan (closing pass, second opinion, HUMAN.md steering, repo context, rubric profiles) depends on them. Recreate as tracked files: tests/mocks/{claude,codex} simulating planner/critic behavior via arg sniffing (acceptEdits / --sandbox workspace-write = planner; otherwise critic) with scripted verdict sequences, and tests/run-matrix.sh running the baseline scenarios (approve-first-round, revise-then-approve, impasse, missing-verdict retry, role pinning, billing-guard refusals) under both VOLLEY_PLANNER values. Baseline must pass against current volley.sh before any feature bead starts.
close_reason: Recreated tests/mocks/{claude,codex} and tests/run-matrix.sh; 39/39 baseline assertions pass under both planner assignments. Commit 2b70e6a.
